contents: Require Import Basics.Utf8 Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.Equivalences.
Require Import Basics.Utf8 Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.Equivalences.
(** We declare a scope for printing [CatEquiv] as [≅] *)

(** * Equivalences in wild categories *)

(** We could define equivalences in any wild 1-category as bi-invertible maps, or in a wild 2-category as half-adjoint equivalences.  However, in concrete cases there is often an equivalent definition of equivalences that we want to use instead, and the important property we need is that it's logically equivalent to (quasi-)isomorphism. In [cat_hasequivs] below, we show that bi-invertible maps do provide a [HasEquivs] structure for any wild 1-category. *)

(** While in recent versions of Rocq, it is ok to have a field of a Record/Class as the source of a coercion, doing so still causes mysterious breakage in the library in at least one spot.  See https://github.com/HoTT/Coq-HoTT/issues/1541.  Things work if we redefine the [CatEquiv'] and [cate_fun'] fields.  For consistency, we redefine all the fields of [HasEquivs] that mention them.  (Only two fields don't mention them, but we redefine them for another reason mentioned below.) *)

Definition CatEquiv {A} `{HasEquivs A} (a b : A)
  := @CatEquiv' A _ _ _ _ _ a b.

Definition cate_fun {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  : a $-> b
  := @cate_fun' A _ _ _ _ _ a b f.

(** Being an equivalence should be a typeclass, but we have to redefine it to work around https://github.com/coq/coq/issues/8994 .  This is almost fixed, but without the wrapper, typeclass search still fails to find hypotheses in the context.  See https://github.com/HoTT/Coq-HoTT/issues/1541.  For consistency, we redefine all fields that mention [CatIsEquiv']. *)

Class CatIsEquiv {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $-> b)
  := catisequiv : CatIsEquiv' a b f.

Instance cate_isequiv {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  : CatIsEquiv f
  := cate_isequiv' a b f.

Definition catie_adjointify {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A}
           (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> a)
           (r : f $o g $== Id b) (s : g $o f $== Id a)
  : CatIsEquiv f
  := catie_adjointify' a b f g r s.

Definition cate_adjointify {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A}
           (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> a)
           (r : f $o g $== Id b) (s : g $o f $== Id a)
  : a $<~> b
  := Build_CatEquiv f (fe:=catie_adjointify f g r s).
(** This one we define to construct the whole inverse equivalence. *)
Definition cate_inv {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b) : b $<~> a.

goals.conclusion: cate_inv' a b f $o f $== Id a
f $o cate_inv' a b f $== Id b

contents: snapply Build_HasEquivs; intros a b; unfold op in a, b; cbn.

goals.conclusion: forall (f : b $-> a) (fe : ?Goal0 f), ?Goal1 (?Goal3 f fe) $== f
forall f : ?Goal, ?Goal1 f $o ?Goal5 f $== Id a
forall f : ?Goal, ?Goal5 f $o ?Goal1 f $== Id b

forall (f : b $-> a) (g : a $-> b),
g $o f $== Id b -> f $o g $== Id a -> ?Goal0 f
forall f : b $<~> a, CatIsEquiv (cate_fun' b a f)
forall f : b $-> a, CatIsEquiv f -> b $<~> a

forall (f : b $-> a) (fe : CatIsEquiv f),
cate_fun' b a (cate_buildequiv' b a f fe) $== f
forall f : b $<~> a, cate_fun' b a f $o cate_inv' b a f $== Id a
forall f : b $<~> a, cate_inv' b a f $o cate_fun' b a f $== Id b

forall (f : b $-> a) (g : a $-> b),
g $o f $== Id b -> f $o g $== Id a -> CatIsEquiv f

contents:
Definition cate_issect {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  : f^-1$ $o f $== Id a.

goals.conclusion: f^-1$ $o f $== cate_inv' a b f $o cate_fun' a b f

contents:
Definition cate_isretr {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  : f $o f^-1$ $== Id b
  := cate_issect (A:=A^op) (b:=a) (a:=b) f.
(** If [g] is a section of an equivalence, then it is the inverse. *)

Definition cate_inverse_sect {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  (g : b $-> a) (p : f $o g $== Id b)
  : cate_fun f^-1$ $== g.
(** If [g] is a retraction of an equivalence, then it is the inverse. *)

Definition cate_inverse_retr {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  (g : b $-> a) (p : g $o f $== Id a)
  : cate_fun f^-1$ $== g
  := cate_inverse_sect (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=a) f g p.
(** It follows that the inverse of the equivalence you get by adjointification is homotopic to the inverse [g] provided. *)

goals.conclusion: cate_adjointify f g r s $o g $== Id b

contents: (** The identity morphism is an equivalence *)

Instance catie_id {A} `{HasEquivs A} (a : A)
  : CatIsEquiv (Id a)
  := catie_adjointify (Id a) (Id a) (cat_idl (Id a)) (cat_idr (Id a)).

Definition id_cate {A} `{HasEquivs A} (a : A)
  : a $<~> a
  := Build_CatEquiv (Id a).
(** Anything homotopic to an equivalence is an equivalence. This should not be an instance. *)
(** Equivalences can be composed.  In order to make use of duality, we factor out parts of the proof as two lemmas. *)

Definition compose_catie_issect {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (g : b $<~> c) (f : a $<~> b)
  : (f^-1$ $o g^-1$ $o (g $o f) $== Id a)
  := compose_catie_isretr (A:=A^op) (a:=c) (b:=b) (c:=a) f g.

Definition id_cate_fun {A} `{HasEquivs A} (a : A)
  : cate_fun (id_cate a) $== Id a.

refine (compose_cate_fun _ f $@ _ $@ cat_assoc f g h $@ _ $@
                           compose_cate_funinv h _).

goals.conclusion: h $oE g $o f $== h $o g $o f
h $oE g $o f $== h $o g $o f

contents: (* We use [exact_no_check] just to save a small amount of time. *)

goals.conclusion: id_cate b $o f $== Id b $o f

contents:
Definition compose_cate_idr {A} `{HasEquivs A}
           {a b : A} (f : a $<~> b)
  : cate_fun (f $oE id_cate a) $== cate_fun f
  := compose_cate_idl (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=a) f.
(** Some more convenient equalities for equivalences. The naming scheme is similar to [PathGroupoids.v].*)

Definition compose_V_hh {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (f : b $<~> c) (g : a $-> b) :
  f^-1$ $o (f $o g) $== g :=
  (cat_assoc_opp _ _ _) $@ (cate_issect f $@R g) $@ cat_idl g.

Definition compose_h_Vh {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (f : c $<~> b) (g : a $-> b) :
  f $o (f^-1$ $o g) $== g :=
  (cat_assoc_opp _ _ _) $@ (cate_isretr f $@R g) $@ cat_idl g.

Definition compose_hh_V {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (f : b $-> c) (g : a $<~> b) :
  (f $o g) $o g^-1$ $== f :=
  cat_assoc _ _ _ $@ (f $@L cate_isretr g) $@ cat_idr f.

Definition compose_hV_h {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (f : b $-> c) (g : b $<~> a) :
  (f $o g^-1$) $o g $== f :=
  cat_assoc _ _ _ $@ (f $@L cate_issect g) $@ cat_idr f.
(** Equivalences are both monomorphisms and epimorphisms (but not the converse). *)

Definition cate_monic_equiv {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (e : a $<~> b)
  : Monic e.

Definition cate_epic_equiv {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (e : a $<~> b)
  : Epic e
  := cate_monic_equiv (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=a) e.

(** ** Movement Lemmas *)

(** These lemmas can be used to move equivalences around in an equation. *)

Definition cate_moveL_eM {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : a $<~> b) (f : a $-> c) (g : b $-> c)
  (p : f $o e^-1$ $== g)
  : f $== g $o e.

Definition cate_moveR_eM {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : b $<~> a) (f : a $-> c) (g : b $-> c)
  (p : f $== g $o e^-1$)
  : f $o e $== g.

Definition cate_moveL_Me {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : b $<~> c) (f : a $-> c) (g : a $-> b)
  (p : e^-1$ $o f $== g)
  : f $== e $o g
  := cate_moveL_eM (A:=A^op) (a:=c) (b:=b) (c:=a) e f g p.

Definition cate_moveR_Me {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : c $<~> b) (f : a $-> c) (g : a $-> b)
  (p : f $== e^-1$ $o g)
  : e $o f $== g
  := cate_moveR_eM (A:=A^op) (a:=c) (b:=b) (c:=a) e f g p.

Definition cate_moveL_eV {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : a $<~> b) (f : b $-> c) (g : a $-> c)
  (p : f $o e $== g)
  : f $== g $o e^-1$.

Definition cate_moveR_eV {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : b $<~> a) (f : b $-> c) (g : a $-> c)
  (p : f $== g $o e)
  : f $o e^-1$ $== g.

Definition cate_moveL_Ve {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : b $<~> c) (f : a $-> b) (g : a $-> c)
  (p : e $o f $== g)
  : f $== e^-1$ $o g
  := cate_moveL_eV (A:=A^op) (a:=c) (b:=b) (c:=a) e f g p.

Definition cate_moveR_Ve {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A}
  (e : b $<~> c) (f : a $-> c) (g : a $-> b)
  (p : f $== e $o g)
  : e^-1$ $o f $== g
  := cate_moveR_eV (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=c) (c:=a) e f g p.

Definition cate_moveL_V1 {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} {e : a $<~> b} (f : b $-> a)
  (p : e $o f $== Id _)
  : f $== cate_fun e^-1$.

Definition cate_moveL_1V {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} {e : a $<~> b} (f : b $-> a)
  (p : f $o e $== Id _)
  : f $== cate_fun e^-1$
  := cate_moveL_V1 (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=a) f p.

Definition cate_moveR_V1 {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} {e : a $<~> b} (f : b $-> a)
  (p : Id _ $== e $o f)
  : cate_fun e^-1$ $== f.

Definition cate_moveR_1V {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} {e : a $<~> b} (f : b $-> a)
  (p : Id _ $== f $o e)
  : cate_fun e^-1$ $== f
  := cate_moveR_V1 (A:=A^op) (a:=b) (b:=a) f p.
(** Lemmas about the underlying map of an equivalence. *)

Definition cate_inv2 {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} {e f : a $<~> b} (p : cate_fun e $== cate_fun f)
  : cate_fun e^-1$ $== cate_fun f^-1$.

Definition cate_inv_compose {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (e : a $<~> b) (f : b $<~> c)
  : cate_fun (f $oE e)^-1$ $== cate_fun (e^-1$ $oE f^-1$).

Definition cate_inv_compose' {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b c : A} (e : a $<~> b) (f : b $<~> c)
  : cate_fun (f $oE e)^-1$ $== e^-1$ $o f^-1$.

Definition cate_inv_V {A} `{HasEquivs A} {a b : A} (e : a $<~> b)
  : cate_fun (e^-1$)^-1$ $== cate_fun e.
(** Any sufficiently coherent functor preserves equivalences.  *)

goals.conclusion: fmap F f $o fmap F f^-1$ $== Id (F b)
fmap F f^-1$ $o fmap F f $== Id (F a)
fmap F f $o fmap F f^-1$ $== Id (F b)

contents: exact ((fmap_comp F f^-1$ f)^$ $@ fmap2 F (cate_isretr _) $@ fmap_id F _).

goals.conclusion: fmap F f^-1$ $o fmap F f $== Id (F a)

contents: exact ((fmap_comp F f f^-1$)^$ $@ fmap2 F (cate_issect _) $@ fmap_id F _).

goals.conclusion: emap F (g $oE f) $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
emap F (g $oE f) $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
fmap F (g $oE f) $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
fmap F (g $o f) $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
emap F (g $oE f) $== emap F g $oE emap F f
emap F (g $oE f) $== emap F g $oE emap F f
fmap F g $o fmap F f $== emap F g $oE emap F f
emap F g $oE emap F f $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
emap F g $o emap F f $== fmap F g $o fmap F f
fmap F e^-1$ $== emap F e^-1$

contents: (** When we have equivalences, we can define what it means for a category to be univalent. *)

Definition cat_equiv_path {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A} (a b : A)
  : (a = b) -> (a $<~> b).

Class IsUnivalent1Cat (A : Type) `{HasEquivs A}
  := isequiv_cat_equiv_path :: forall a b, IsEquiv (@cat_equiv_path A _ _ _ _ _ a b).

Definition cat_path_equiv {A : Type} `{IsUnivalent1Cat A} (a b : A)
  : (a $<~> b) -> (a = b)
  := (cat_equiv_path a b)^-1.

Instance isgraph_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : IsGraph (core A).
intros a b ; exact (uncore a $<~> uncore b).

Instance is01cat_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : Is01Cat (core A).

goals.conclusion: forall a : core A, CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore a)

forall a b c : core A,
CatEquiv' (uncore b) (uncore c) ->
CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore b) -> CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore c)
forall a : core A, CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore a)

forall a b c : core A,
CatEquiv' (uncore b) (uncore c) ->
CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore b) -> CatEquiv' (uncore a) (uncore c)

contents: intros a b c ; exact compose_cate.

Instance is2graph_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : Is2Graph (core A).
intros f g ; exact (cate_fun f $== cate_fun g).

Instance is01cat_core_hom {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A} (a b : core A)
  : Is01Cat (a $-> b).

goals.conclusion: forall a0 : a $-> b, a0 $-> a0
forall a0 : a $-> b, a0 $-> a0

contents: intros f g h; cbn; exact cat_comp.

Instance is0gpd_core_hom {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A} (a b : core A)
  : Is0Gpd (a $-> b).

goals.conclusion: forall a0 b0 : a $-> b, (a0 $-> b0) -> b0 $-> a0

(cate_fun' (uncore a) (uncore b) f $-> cate_fun' (uncore a) (uncore b) g) ->
cate_fun' (uncore a) (uncore b) g $-> cate_fun' (uncore a) (uncore b) f

forall a0 b0 : a $-> b,
(a0 $-> b0) -> cat_postcomp a h a0 $-> cat_postcomp a h b0

contents: intros f g al; cbn in h.

goals.conclusion: cat_postcomp a h f $-> cat_postcomp a h g

contents:
exact (compose_cate_fun h f
           $@ (h $@L al)
           $@ (compose_cate_fun h g)^$).

goals.conclusion:
forall a0 b0 : b $-> c,
(a0 $-> b0) -> cat_precomp c h a0 $-> cat_precomp c h b0

contents: intros f g al; cbn in h.

goals.conclusion: cat_precomp c h f $-> cat_precomp c h g

contents: (** TODO: Why can't Coq resolve this? *)

refine (compose_cate_fun f h
           $@ (_ $@R h)
           $@ (compose_cate_fun g h)^$).

Instance is1cat_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : Is1Cat (core A).

goals.conclusion:
forall (a b c d : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c) 
(h : c $-> d), h $o g $o f $== h $o (g $o f)

forall (a b c d : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c) 
(h : c $-> d), h $o (g $o f) $== h $o g $o f
forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b), Id b $o f $== f
forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b), f $o Id a $== f

forall (a b c d : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c) 
(h : c $-> d), h $o g $o f $== h $o (g $o f)

forall (a b c d : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c) 
(h : c $-> d), h $o (g $o f) $== h $o g $o f
forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b), Id b $o f $== f
forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b), f $o Id a $== f

contents:
Instance is0gpd_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : Is0Gpd (core A).

goals.conclusion: forall a b : core A, (a $-> b) -> b $-> a

contents: intros a b f; cbn in *; exact (f^-1$).

Instance is1gpd_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : Is1Gpd (core A).

apply Build_Is1Gpd; cbn ; intros a b f;
    refine (compose_cate_fun _ _ $@ _ $@ (id_cate_fun _)^$).

Instance hasequivs_core {A : Type} `{HasEquivs A}
  : HasEquivs (core A).

goals.conclusion: forall a b : core A, (a $-> b) -> Type
forall a b : core A, ?CatEquiv' a b -> a $-> b

forall (a b : core A) (f : ?CatEquiv' a b),
?CatIsEquiv' a b (?cate_fun' a b f)
forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b), ?CatIsEquiv' a b f -> ?CatEquiv' a b

forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b) (fe : ?CatIsEquiv' a b f),
?cate_fun' a b (?cate_buildequiv' a b f fe) $== f
forall a b : core A, ?CatEquiv' a b -> b $-> a

forall (a b : core A) (f : ?CatEquiv' a b),
?cate_inv' a b f $o ?cate_fun' a b f $== Id a

forall (a b : core A) (f : ?CatEquiv' a b),
?cate_fun' a b f $o ?cate_inv' a b f $== Id b

forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> a),
f $o g $== Id b -> g $o f $== Id a -> ?CatIsEquiv' a b f
forall a b : core A, (a $-> b) -> Type
forall a b : core A, (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b -> a $-> b

forall (a b : core A) (f : (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b),
?CatIsEquiv' a b (?cate_fun' a b f)

forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b),
?CatIsEquiv' a b f -> (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b

forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b) (fe : ?CatIsEquiv' a b f),
?cate_fun' a b (?cate_buildequiv' a b f fe) $== f
forall a b : core A, (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b -> b $-> a

forall (a b : core A) (f : (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b),
?cate_inv' a b f $o ?cate_fun' a b f $== Id a

forall (a b : core A) (f : (fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b),
?cate_fun' a b f $o ?cate_inv' a b f $== Id b

forall (a b : core A) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> a),
f $o g $== Id b -> g $o f $== Id a -> ?CatIsEquiv' a b f

contents: all: intros a b f; cbn; intros.

(fun a0 b0 : core A => a0 $-> b0) a b

forall (x y : core A) (f g : uncore x $<~> uncore y), IsEquiv (ap cate_fun)

forall (x y : core A) (f0 g0 : uncore x $<~> uncore y), IsEquiv (ap cate_fun)

contents: (** ** Initial objects and terminal objects are all respectively equivalent. *)

Lemma cate_isinitial A `{HasEquivs A} (x y : A)
  : IsInitial x -> IsInitial y -> x $<~> y.

goals.conclusion: IsInitial x -> IsInitial y -> x $<~> y
IsInitial x -> IsInitial y -> x $<~> y

contents:
Definition cate_isterminal A `{HasEquivs A} (x y : A)
  : IsTerminal x -> IsTerminal y -> y $<~> x
  := cate_isinitial A^op x y.

Lemma isinitial_cate A `{HasEquivs A} (x y : A)
  : x $<~> y -> IsInitial x -> IsInitial y.

goals.conclusion: x $<~> y -> IsInitial x -> IsInitial y
x $<~> y -> IsInitial x -> IsInitial y
{f0 : y $-> z & forall g : y $-> z, f0 $== g}
forall g : y $-> z, (inx z).1 $o f^-1$ $== g

contents:
Definition isterminal_cate A `{HasEquivs A} (x y : A)
  : y $<~> x -> IsTerminal x -> IsTerminal y
  := isinitial_cate A^op x y.

(** ** There is a default notion of equivalence for a 1-category, namely bi-invertibility. *)

(** We do not use the half-adjoint definition, since we can't prove adjointification for that definition. *)
Arguments Build_Cat_IsBiInv {A}%_type_scope {_ _ _ _ x y f} cat_equiv_inv cat_eisretr cat_equiv_inv' cat_eissect'.
(** The two inverses are necessarily homotopic. *)

Definition cat_inverses_homotopic {A} `{Is1Cat A} {x y : A} (f : x $-> y) {bif : Cat_IsBiInv f}
  : cat_equiv_inv f $== cat_equiv_inv' f.

goals.conclusion: Id x $o cat_equiv_inv f $== cat_equiv_inv' f
cat_equiv_inv' f $o f $o cat_equiv_inv f $== cat_equiv_inv' f
cat_equiv_inv' f $o (f $o cat_equiv_inv f) $== cat_equiv_inv' f
cat_equiv_inv' f $o Id y $== cat_equiv_inv' f

contents: (** Therefore we can prove [eissect] for the first inverse as well. *)
(** This shows that any 1-category satisfies [HasEquivs].  We do not make it an instance, since we may want to use a different [HasEquivs] structure in particular cases. *)
Definition cat_hasequivs A `{Is1Cat A} : HasEquivs A.

goals.conclusion: (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y -> x $-> y

forall f : (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y,

  ((fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal1@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f)

(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y

(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal1@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y
  ((fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal3@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f fe) $==
f
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y -> y $-> x

forall f : (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y,
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal5@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $o
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal1@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $== 
Id x

forall f : (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y,
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal1@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $o
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal5@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $== 
Id y

forall (f : x $-> y) (g : y $-> x),
f $o g $== Id y ->
g $o f $== Id x -> (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal0@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f
(fun x0 y0 : A => Cat_BiInv A x0 y0) x y -> x $-> y

forall f : (fun x0 y0 : A => Cat_BiInv A x0 y0) x y,
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y
  ((fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal0@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f)

forall f : x $-> y,
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f ->
(fun x0 y0 : A => Cat_BiInv A x0 y0) x y

forall (f : x $-> y) (fe : (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f),
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal0@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y
  ((fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal2@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f fe) $==
f
(fun x0 y0 : A => Cat_BiInv A x0 y0) x y -> y $-> x

(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal4@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $o
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal0@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $== 
Id x

forall f : (fun x0 y0 : A => Cat_BiInv A x0 y0) x y,
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal0@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $o
(fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal4@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f $== 
Id y

forall (f : x $-> y) (g : y $-> x),
f $o g $== Id y ->
g $o f $== Id x -> (fun x0 y0 : A => ?Goal@{x:=x0; y:=y0}) x y f
forall g : y $-> x, f $o g $== Id y -> g $o f $== Id x -> ?Goal
Cat_IsBiInv f -> Cat_BiInv A x y

forall fe : Cat_IsBiInv f,
{| cat_equiv_fun := f; cat_equiv_isequiv := fe |} $== f
forall g : y $-> x, f $o g $== Id y -> g $o f $== Id x -> Cat_IsBiInv f
